markdownRemark.html:
Notes

Here is where I compile all of the notes I take for every conceivable thing in my life. I'm a huge fan of organization and simplification, so that's my ultimate goal in compiling all of this knowledge.

Components

  • Money Umbrella - Everything related to making money and business
  • Christ and the church - Everything related to the enjoyment of Christ and the church practically
  • Learning & Building - Various notes on things I'm actually improving in and creating stuff in
  • Charisma Umbrella - Notes on building up charisma umbrella

Knowledge Stuff to Simplify

  • What is an API? (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=s7wmiS2mSXY)
  • How does the Internet and webpages work?
  • How do software applications actually work? (frontend and backend)
  • Dynamic and asynchronous JavaScript and how web requests and http and all the mumbo jumbo
  • Guide for music recording and editing (vocal mixing) -> different effects like compressor, delay, reverb
  • What is context switching?
  • Guide to ES6 JavaScript
  • Master digital marketing plan (attention investing) -> (https://medium.com/better-marketing/gary-vaynerchuks-attention-strategy-f4211352aac6)
  • What is the best algorithm for talking to anyone (getting their #)

List of Topics to Simplify

  • What does it really mean to dispense Christ? (the reality is in Jesus' humanity)
  • The prereq to knowing God's will (He doesn't play games) -> (John 7:17)
  • How to deal with the impulses of the soul (must be trained ahead of time)
  • Depth of roots are tested in times of turmoil
  • What does it really mean to suffer? To bear the cross? What separated the Lord from the criminals?
  • The Lord doesn't want us to merely preach the gospel, He wants us to BECOME the gospel
  • The amazing account & picture of Noah and his family (gaining 3 and 3)
  • God does everything by His word (it drives the universe)
  • Learning how to experience the Lord as the Man of sorrows (burdens everywhere -> blessed are those who mourn)

  • Lessons on caring (continued shepherding) from time with Mike Wong

    • When you bring someone to the Lord, you can't leave him/her alone. It's too dangerous. The enemy will come in to attack right away. They need to be IMMEDIATELY plugged into the church life in a strong way.
  • I truly am the mdoern incarnation of Jeremiah
  • Track the significance of households in the Bible
  • Stories of Kenny (reached the point where just his presence can trigger feelings of unrest and tension) and Susan clearly show what is NOT useful to the Lord (natural zeal and/or outward spirituality)

  • The church life primarily in the homes (the reality of the church life) -> sisters' function

    • The household is the real unit for the church life and for serving the Lord
  • Musing on the 3 chapters in both old and new testaments that God finally had what he wanted, and how man fell from it (looseness regarding marriage and hypocrisy)
  • Musing on what a friend or companion really is and why I'm happy despite having a small circle of "friends"
  • Important principle of exponential returns -> lots of time is spent figuring things out and getting things prepared, and people only see the output but they don't see the time put in before hand
  • What makes God angry?
  • What is the "one new man" actually?
  • [Colossian 3] - we have one life with the Lord

  • Encapsulation of entire Bible from various viewpoints

    • Type and reality of the children of Israel
    • Type and reality of the good land
    • The divine marriage
  • Great picture of the kingdom -> two views (life and authority) -> country within a country
  • Seeing the law from God's view
  • How can our love for the Lord increase?
  • Who is the Triune God really?

  • Why marriage is supremely important

    • Why I'm experiencing PB & J

  • What is the world?

    • What are the desires and needs ordained by God, and what was added by Satan (self and flesh (lust, vainglory of life)) -> understanding this helps us understand what it means to deny the self
  • What is prayer?
  • What ae the general principles of the church life?
  • What is the difference between living for the Lord and living to the Lord?
  • Explore the geography of the good land
  • Really learn what are the factors that turn off young people (especially parents)
  • Musing on the clear damage of "sub-optimal" parenting - Jieni and the other YP
  • Musing on one of the toughest things to do: say nothing (only the humanity of Jesus can do this)
  • So many people "care about" others, yet few people "care for" others -> really tending for every aspect of their being (every person's essential needs)

  • What people really lack are families (physical or spiritual) that are in resurrection -> no judgment or hypocrisy or standards and full of love and proper discipline

    • Musing on why young people leave the church life (66% of Christians aged 22-30 left)

      • 4 reasons: weren't trained to handle the transitions, hypocrisy (especially in parents0, judgement, didn't have real intergenerational relationships (didn't feel the family life)
      • People who are solid in the church life tend to have around 5 meaningful intergenerational relationships (besides parents and serving ones)

    • What causes people to leave the Lord and the church (given that there is a desire within all of us to be with the saints)

      • Categories: offenses, hidden rocks, accusations from the enemy, lack of care or outreach, lack of feeling (turning to the world)

  • What are the litmus tests for our true spiritual condition?

    • fruit-bearing
    • heart for all mankind
    • service
    • character
    • bearing the cross
    • what is the order of our priorities
